name: azure-hosted-copilot-sdk
description: Build and deploy GitHub Copilot SDK apps to Azure. USE FOR: build copilot app, create copilot app, copilot SDK, @github/copilot-sdk, scaffold copilot project, copilot-powered app, deploy copilot app, host on azure, azure model, BYOM, bring your own model, use my own model, azure openai model, DefaultAzureCredential, self-hosted model, copilot SDK service, chat app with copilot, copilot-sdk-service template, azd init copilot, CopilotClient, createSession, sendAndWait, GitHub Models API. DO NOT USE FOR: using Copilot (not building with it), Copilot Extensions, Azure Functions without Copilot, general web apps without copilot SDK, Foundry agent hosting (use microsoft-foundry skill), agent evaluation (use microsoft-foundry skill).
capabilities: Scaffold new Copilot SDK projects using azure-samples/copilot-sdk-service template
Integrate Copilot SDK into existing Node.js, Python, Go, or .NET applications
Configure Azure BYOM (Bring Your Own Model) with DefaultAzureCredential authentication
Deploy Copilot-powered apps to Azure Container Apps with Azure Developer CLI
Set up GitHub token flow with Key Vault for secure credential management
Discover available models via listModels() and Azure CLI deployment queries
author: microsoft
securitySummary: This Microsoft skill contains only Markdown documentation files with instructional bash commands for Azure deployment workflows. All 194 static findings are false positives: backtick patterns are documentation examples (not code execution), URLs are official Microsoft/GitHub references, and environment variable access is legitimate for Azure credential management. No executable code or malicious patterns detected.
valueStatement: Developers struggle to build and host AI-powered apps with GitHub Copilot SDK. This skill scaffolds projects, integrates Copilot SDK into existing codebases, configures Azure models, and deploys to Azure Container Apps.
userTitle: Deploy Copilot SDK Apps to Azure
limitations: Requires Docker Desktop installed and running for local development
Requires GitHub CLI with copilot scope for authentication
Azure BYOM only supports o-series and gpt-5 family models (not gpt-4o)
Bearer tokens expire after 1 hour and require manual refresh in production

useCases.targetUser: Developer building a new AI-powered chat application
useCases.title: Greenfield Copilot App Development
useCases.description: Start a new project with Copilot SDK integration from scratch

useCases.targetUser: Team lead modernizing an existing codebase with AI features
useCases.title: Add Copilot to Existing Application
useCases.description: Integrate Copilot SDK endpoints into an existing web application without rewriting infrastructure

useCases.targetUser: Enterprise architect requiring compliant self-hosted AI infrastructure
useCases.title: Enterprise Azure Model Deployment

promptTemplates.title: Scaffold a new Copilot SDK project
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new Copilot SDK project on Azure. I want to build a chat application from scratch with React UI and Express API.

promptTemplates.title: Integrate Copilot SDK into existing app
promptTemplates.prompt: Add Copilot SDK to my existing Express.js application. I have a /api/routes folder and want to add an AI chat endpoint at /api/chat.

promptTemplates.title: Configure Azure BYOM with specific model
promptTemplates.prompt: Set up Azure BYOM for my Copilot SDK app. I have an Azure OpenAI resource with an o4-mini deployment at https://myresource.openai.azure.com. Use managed identity authentication.

promptTemplates.title: Deploy existing Copilot SDK app to Azure
promptTemplates.prompt: I have a working Copilot SDK app locally. Help me deploy it to Azure Container Apps with proper infrastructure using azd.

bestPractices: Read AGENTS.md in scaffolded templates to understand file purposes before modifying
Use context7 MCP tools to get current Copilot SDK documentation and code examples
Increase nginx proxy_read_timeout to 300s for multi-turn AI sessions to prevent 504 errors
antiPatterns: Do not run azd init inside existing projects - it overwrites files. Scaffold to temp directory first.
Do not use wireApi 'responses' with store:false - it breaks multi-turn tool calls. Use 'completions' instead.
Do not nest apiVersion at top level for Azure provider - it must be under azure: { apiVersion: '...' }

faq.question: What models work with Azure BYOM in Copilot SDK?
faq.answer: Only o-series (o3, o3-mini, o4-mini) and gpt-5 family models support BYOM. Models like gpt-4o and gpt-4.1 do not work because they do not support the SDK's encrypted content format.

faq.question: How do I fix 'gh auth token' failures?
faq.answer: Run 'gh auth login' to authenticate, then 'gh auth refresh --scopes copilot' to add the required copilot scope. GitHub CLI must be installed.

faq.question: Why does my Copilot SDK session hang or timeout?
faq.answer: Check nginx.conf.template and increase proxy_read_timeout to 300s. The default 60s timeout causes 504 errors for long-running AI sessions.

faq.question: How do I discover available Azure model deployments?
faq.answer: Use 'az cognitiveservices account deployment list --name <resource> --resource-group <rg>'. The SDK listModels() only returns GitHub models, not Azure deployments.

faq.question: What is the difference between GitHub default and Azure BYOM?
faq.answer: GitHub default uses GitHub's hosted models with automatic authentication. Azure BYOM lets you use your own Azure OpenAI or AI Foundry deployments with DefaultAzureCredential for enterprise control.

faq.question: How do I handle token expiration for Azure BYOM?
faq.answer: Bearer tokens expire after 1 hour. For production, get a fresh token per request using DefaultAzureCredential.getToken(). Do not cache tokens for long-running sessions.
